name: okpack-threat-landscape-annual-reports
_note: THE seed. Full-TEXT ingest of ALL categories of the awesome-annual-security-reports corpus (MIT) -> source pages stamped with an inferred report_theme + vendor. Default: fetch from PUBLIC GitHub, bounded (recent years + cap 120). Operator override: set ANNUAL_REPORTS_DIR in .env to a local checkout. no_agent for the import (zero-token) — large source pages, so the trend/metric AGENT lanes spend when compiling. See docs/data-sources.md.
prompt: Ingest annual security reports (all categories, full text, themed). Pure script, wakeAgent=false, ZERO LLM tokens for the import. Runs FIRST in the weekly chain (Mon 12:07, before the hour-13 analysis lanes) so theme-trends/vendor-index/landscape-shifts see the fresh import same-week — NOT @jitter (independent jitter made the import land after the analysis, so new reports lagged a week).

name: okpack-threat-landscape-theme-trends
_note: Cross-report THEME emergence: counts reports per report_theme per year -> rising/fading trend pages (the landscape analog of TTP-diffusion). Reads the report_theme+year frontmatter the ingest stamps. no_agent, ZERO LLM tokens.
prompt: Synthesize theme-emergence trends across the report corpus. Pure script, wakeAgent=false, ZERO LLM tokens.

name: okpack-threat-landscape-theme-news-velocity
_note: Per-theme NEWS velocity: counts recent security-news sources (the deployment's raw news drop) whose text matches each theme, this window vs prior, and grafts a news_velocity block onto trends/theme-*.md — PRESERVING theme_trends' annual-report count_by_year. DAILY so the signal tracks the news, not the annual-report cadence. no_agent, ZERO LLM tokens.
prompt: Graft per-theme news velocity onto the theme trend pages. Pure script, wakeAgent=false, ZERO LLM tokens.

name: okpack-threat-landscape-vendor-index
_note: Builds a publisher page per report publisher (report count, themes, years, links) — the who-says-what-about-what map. no_agent, ZERO LLM tokens.
prompt: Build the publisher coverage map from the report corpus. Pure script, wakeAgent=false, ZERO LLM tokens.

name: okpack-threat-landscape-feed-fetch
_note: Optional ongoing signal (empty by default; ZERO upstream calls until you populate feeds.opml). The annual reports are the primary source. @jitter:2h expanded to a random minute at install.
prompt: okpack-threat-landscape feed pull: fetch the threat-landscape OPML into raw/threat-landscape/ for the ingest lane. Pure script, wakeAgent=false.

name: okpack-threat-landscape-weekly-brief
_note: Agent lane. Synthesizes the week's landscape brief from recent trends + reports. Spends model tokens. Writes briefings/weekly-<date>.md. Runs LAST in the weekly chain (Mon 15:20, after landscape-shifts at 14:45) so the brief reflects the day's computed shifts — NOT @jitter (it kept landing before the shifts).
prompt: Weekly threat-landscape brief. FIRST, two tool calls: file_read wiki/HOT.md, then file_read the wiki/trends/theme-*.md pages. Each theme page carries BOTH an annual-report `count_by_year` (slow structural coverage) and a `news_velocity` block (recent security-news source count THIS 7-day window vs the prior 7 days, with direction + per-day rate). Write the brief via mcp_okengine_write_create_entity (the enforced write path — never file_write) to "briefings/weekly-<YYYY-MM-DD>.md" (frontmatter: type: briefing, title, published: <TODAY YYYY-MM-DD>, updated: <TODAY> — use TODAY's real date, not a guess). LEAD with the NEWS PULSE: rank the themes by news_velocity this week (highest /day first), call out which are RISING or COOLING vs the prior week, then note where the slow annual-report backdrop DIVERGES from the news (a theme flat in vendor reports but hot in news volume, or vice versa — the divergence IS the finding). Each theme 2-3 sentences with [[trends/theme-...]] wikilinks. Where vendors disagree on a metric, say so (the range is the finding). Expert-grade, terse; skip 101 explanation. LOCAL-ONLY: do not use web tools.

name: okpack-threat-landscape-landscape-shifts
prompt: Weekly dated landscape-shift digest (Mon 14:45 local, after theme-trends refreshes): diffs trends/theme-* metrics vs the previous shift snapshot and writes trends/shift-YYYY-MM-DD.md for the cockpit's date-keyed shifts stream. Pure script, wakeAgent=false.
